description: Unified playback health, roster-driven: every panel keyed off the stream_providers roster. Roster table (Postgres) anchors who exists + their lifecycle (status/group/scraper_operated); per-provider health, parser latency, real-user telemetry and HLS-proxy panels cover all 13 providers. $provider enumerates the full roster via provider_info.

panels.description: Full player roster tri-state tally, from the provider_info `status` label: 'Enabled' = in the normal auto-failover / always-on set; 'Degraded' = registered + manually selectable but out of auto-failover (e.g. AnimeFever ad-substitution, AUTO-484); 'Total' = every registered provider. Covers the EN scraper chain, Kodik, AniLib, Hanime, Raw (JP), 18anime and the self-hosted ae library. Enabled + Degraded + Disabled = Total — see the Provider Roster table below for the per-provider tri-state.
panels.title: Providers (Enabled / Degraded / Total)

panels.description: Fraction of provider×stage probes currently UP (all providers: Kodik, 18anime + EN scraper chain). 1.0 = all healthy.
panels.title: Stage Health

panels.description: Age of the last SUCCESSFUL scraper playability canary run. Shows 'never' (not a 56-year epoch artifact) when the canary has never succeeded. Red if stale (canary runs daily).
panels.title: Probe Last Run (age)

panels.description: Total provider→provider failover events in the EN scraper chain over the selected time range (top time picker).
panels.title: Fallbacks

panels.description: Merged provider roster (Postgres stream_providers — source of truth) + live playback verdict (daily honest probe). One row per provider: the derived State (UP · Recovering · Degrading = one failed probe, pending confirmation · Down = confirmed failing · Disabled = admin-disabled only — the health lifecycle from policy+health; a parked manual provider shows its LIVE health (the auto/manual split is the far-right In auto-failover chain column), same scheme as the Provider State History timeline), the probed Playability (up/degraded/down — what we measured), Last tick (age since this provider was last probed; 'never' = never probed), how each stream is Resolved via (scraper microservice + engine — 'Camoufox' = stealth browser sidecar, 'HTTP' = direct fetch — vs catalog parser, FE iframe, or self-hosted), the top-3 failure Reasons with the anime title each failed on, and (far right) whether the provider is In auto-failover chain — yes (auto) · no (manual) · disabled. Non-probed providers (Kodik/AniLib/Hanime/Raw/18anime/ae) show '— not probed'. Filtered by $provider for the probe columns.

targets.rawSql: SELECT name AS provider, "group" AS "Group", CASE policy WHEN 'auto' THEN 'yes (auto)' WHEN 'manual' THEN 'no (manual)' ELSE 'disabled' END AS "In auto-failover chain", health AS "Health", CASE WHEN policy = 'disabled' THEN 'Disabled' WHEN health = 'up' THEN 'UP' WHEN health = 'recovering' THEN 'Recovering' WHEN health = 'degraded' THEN 'Degrading' ELSE 'Down' END AS "State", last_probed_at AS "Last probed", EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - last_probed_at))::bigint AS "Last tick", CASE WHEN scraper_operated AND engine = 'browser' THEN 'scraper · Camoufox' WHEN scraper_operated THEN 'scraper · HTTP' WHEN name = 'ae' THEN 'self-hosted' WHEN name = 'kodik-iframe' THEN 'FE iframe' ELSE 'catalog parser' END AS "Resolved via", description AS "Description", ai_probe_notes AS "AI Probe Notes", (CASE WHEN name = 'kodik-iframe' THEN 4 WHEN "group" = 'en' THEN 1 WHEN "group" = 'ru' THEN 2 WHEN "group" = 'adult' THEN 3 WHEN "group" = 'firstparty' THEN 5 ELSE 6 END + CASE WHEN policy = 'disabled' THEN 100 ELSE 0 END) AS sort_rank FROM stream_providers ORDER BY sort_rank, name

SELECT provider, concat(multiIf(countIf(playable = 1) > 0, 'up', countIf(stage = 'playback') > 0, 'degraded', 'down'), ' (', toString(countIf(playable = 1)), '/', toString(count()), ')') AS playability, max(run_ts) AS probed_at, arrayStringConcat(topKIf(3)(label, playable = 0), ' · ') AS reasons FROM (  SELECT provider, stage, playable, run_ts,          concat(reason, ' — ', if(empty(anime_name), 'anime', anime_name)) AS label   FROM probe_runs WHERE (provider, run_ts) IN (SELECT provider, max(run_ts) FROM probe_runs WHERE run_ts > now() - INTERVAL 48 HOUR GROUP BY provider)) GROUP BY provider ORDER BY provider

panels.title: Provider Roster & Playability
Last Tick Metrics
panels.description: Summary of each provider's most recent probe tick: warmup (browser cold-solve), resolve + validate latency, first-segment throughput, CDN host and quality.

targets.rawSql: SELECT name AS "Provider", round((last_tick_metrics::jsonb->>'warmup_ms')::numeric/1000,1) AS "Warmup s", round((last_tick_metrics::jsonb->>'resolve_ms')::numeric/1000,2) AS "Resolve s", round((last_tick_metrics::jsonb->>'validate_ms')::numeric/1000,2) AS "Validate s", round((last_tick_metrics::jsonb->>'throughput_kbps')::numeric/1000,1) AS "Speed Mbps", last_tick_metrics::jsonb->>'cdn_host' AS "CDN", last_tick_metrics::jsonb->>'quality' AS "Quality", (last_tick_metrics::jsonb->>'at')::timestamptz AS "Last tick" FROM stream_providers WHERE last_tick_metrics <> '' AND last_tick_metrics IS NOT NULL ORDER BY "group", name;

panels.description: Per-provider health lifecycle state over time (provider_health_state), defaulting to the last 7 days regardless of the dashboard time picker. Reads the same 5-state scheme as the roster's State column — UP · Recovering · Degrading (one failed probe, pending confirmation) · Down (confirmed failing) · Disabled (admin-disabled only; a parked manual provider shows its LIVE health here, not Disabled). Catalog re-sets the gauge live on every probe-result transition, so each colored segment is a real state change; never-probed rows (ae/kodik/legacy/disabled) show a flat baseline band. Filtered by $provider. The `group!=""` matcher is REQUIRED: it drops any label-less series so a stale or mis-scraped row can't draw a duplicate, frozen (wrong-state) band per provider. Do not remove it.
panels.title: Provider State History

panels.description: Catalog-parser request success rate per provider (success / total) over a 5m window. Providers: allanime, 18anime, animelib, hanime, kodik. NOT the EN scraper provider set.
panels.title: Parser Success Rate % (by provider)

panels.description: Catalog-parser p95 request latency per provider over a 5m window.
panels.title: Parser p95 Latency (by provider)

panels.description: Which providers the failover chain moved AWAY from over the selected range — i.e. how often each provider failed and triggered a fallback. Higher = less reliable as a primary. One bar per provider.
panels.title: Fallbacked from

panels.description: Where the failover chain landed over the selected range — the next provider tried after a fallback. '(chain exhausted)' means no further provider was available (total failure for that attempt). One bar per provider.
panels.title: Stopped fallbacking at

panels.description: Failing (provider, server, reason, anime slot) tuples by cumulative fail count. Single source of truth for canary failures. The daily canary increments rarely, so this uses cumulative counts (not a decaying rate window) to stay populated. Empty = no canary failures ever recorded. Filtered by $provider.

targets.expr: topk(15, sum by (provider, server, reason, slot) (probe_runs_total{result="fail", provider=~"$provider"}))

panels.title: Top Failing (provider, server, reason, slot) Tuples

targets.rawSql: SELECT target AS provider, round(countIf(JSONExtractBool(properties,'reached_playback'))/count(),3) AS reached_rate, count() AS resolves FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind='player_resolve' GROUP BY provider ORDER BY resolves DESC

SELECT target AS provider, round(100*countIf(JSONExtractString(properties,'outcome')='ok')/count(),1) AS ok_pct, count() AS resolves FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind='player_resolve' GROUP BY provider ORDER BY resolves DESC
SELECT target AS provider, quantile(0.95)(duration_ms) AS p95_ms FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind='player_resolve' GROUP BY provider ORDER BY p95_ms DESC

SELECT target AS provider, count() AS stalls, quantile(0.95)(duration_ms) AS p95_stall_ms FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind='player_stall' GROUP BY provider ORDER BY stalls DESC

SELECT target AS provider, JSONExtractString(properties,'error_kind') AS error_kind, count() AS fails FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind='player_resolve' AND JSONExtractString(properties,'outcome')='fail' GROUP BY provider, error_kind ORDER BY fails DESC LIMIT 20

panels.description: HLS proxy upstream fetch rate. 'on-prem' = manifests + segments served from self-hosted MinIO (the ae provider); 'external CDN' = all other providers. Direct on-prem-vs-external load comparison.
panels.title: HLS Proxy Requests/s — on-prem vs external

panels.description: Bytes streamed to clients through the HLS proxy, split on-prem (ae/MinIO) vs external CDN. The on-prem series is the self-hosted server bandwidth load.
panels.title: HLS Proxy Egress — on-prem vs external

panels.description: End-to-end HLS proxy request latency (manifest + segment fetches across all providers; the ae provider's on-prem reads dominate when self-hosted playback is active).
panels.title: HLS Proxy Latency (p50 / p95)

panels.description: Per-provider real-user usage over the selected range. 'Times requested' = player_resolve events (each time the player asked this provider to resolve a stream). 'Restreamed (s)' = total wall-clock seconds streamed through the HLS proxy for this provider (sum of egress session durations; provider-attributed at manifest-mint). Providers with no attributable egress (Kodik iframe, direct embeds) show 0 restream seconds. Empty-provider egress rows are excluded.

targets.rawSql: SELECT prov AS "Provider", countIf(kind = 'player_resolve') AS "Times requested", round(sumIf(duration_ms, kind = 'egress') / 1000) AS "Restreamed (s)" FROM ( SELECT effect_kind AS kind, duration_ms, multiIf(effect_kind = 'egress', provider, target) AS prov FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind IN ('player_resolve', 'egress') ) WHERE prov != '' GROUP BY prov ORDER BY "Times requested" DESC, "Restreamed (s)" DESC

panels.title: Provider Usage — times requested & restream seconds

panels.description: Segment-volume-weighted share of each negotiated protocol over the selected window.

targets.rawSql: SELECT JSONExtractString(properties,'protocol') AS protocol, sum(JSONExtractUInt(properties,'segments')) AS segments FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind='player_protocol' GROUP BY protocol ORDER BY segments DESC

panels.title: Protocol usage share

panels.description: One row per (session × protocol tier): what the ladder delivered on each tier a viewer used. Anonymous.

targets.rawSql: SELECT timestamp AS "Time", JSONExtractString(properties,'protocol') AS "Protocol", coalesce(nullIf(JSONExtractString(properties,'anime_name'),''), anime_id) AS "Anime", JSONExtractString(properties,'combo') AS "Combo", JSONExtractUInt(properties,'segments') AS "Segments", round(JSONExtractFloat(properties,'avg_mbps'),2) AS "Avg Mbps", round(JSONExtractFloat(properties,'dropped_frames_pct'),2) AS "Dropped frames %", JSONExtractUInt(properties,'seg_timeouts') AS "Seg timeouts", round(JSONExtractFloat(properties,'needed_mbps'),2) AS "Needed Mbps", JSONExtractString(properties,'trail') AS "Ladder trail", JSONExtractString(properties,'probe') AS "h3 probe", target AS "Provider" FROM events WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND effect_kind='player_protocol' ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 500

panels.title: Protocol usage (per session × tier)

title: Playback / Health
